title: aquilegia vulgaris subsp. longisepala
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. longisepala, commonly known as the long-spurred columbine, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, with the roots and stems being particularly valued for their medicinal properties. The health benefits of Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. longisepala include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic and reduce blood pressure. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve infusions or decoctions of the roots and stems, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments.
